content:
# Depth-Aware Grading Decision: Ambiance+Depth Preferred (2026-04-01)

## Decision
User prefers RAUNE-Net + Depth-Aware Ambiance over LAB+Depth and flat variants. Key reasons: better colors, higher clarity.

## Why Ambiance+Depth Over LAB+Depth
- Colors feel more natural/appealing despite lower R/G ratio (0.880 vs 0.931)
- Higher perceived clarity from the local contrast (unsharp mask on L channel)
- Near-zero clipping (0.21% white, 0.02% black) — safest for downstream editing
- The smooth tone curves (sigmoid, tanh) produce more pleasing tonal transitions than LAB grade's hard S-curve

## Why Ambiance+Depth Over Ambiance Flat
- Depth-graduated warmth: near subjects warmer, far water stays blue
- Better saturation (0.284 vs 0.260) — depth weighting concentrates color boost where it matters
- Lower noise (+45% vs +56%) — less processing on far/uniform water areas

## Full Pipeline
1. RAUNE-Net inference (36.7M params, ~7.5s CPU / ~0.5s GPU)
2. Load Depth Anything V2 depth map (16-bit, relative depth)
3. Depth-aware ambiance grading with graduated parameters:
   - Near (depth~1): warmth_a=6, warmth_b=4, vibrance=0.9, strength=0.6
   - Far (depth~0): warmth_a=1, warmth_b=0, vibrance=0.4, strength=0.3
   - Highlight compression stronger on far (protect bright water)
   - Local contrast (clarity) applied globally

## Metrics (averaged across 3 test frames)
| Metric | Value |
|--------|-------|
| R/G ratio | 0.880 |
| B/G ratio | 1.132 |
| Saturation | 0.284 |
| White clip | 0.21% |
| Black clip | 0.02% |
| Noise delta | +45% |
| Grading time | ~0.18s/frame |

## Script
working/sea_thru_poc/run_raune_depth_grade.py — function `depth_aware_ambiance()`
